ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Feb-23-2017 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Default Judgment
Off Calendar Feb-16-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 514, Judge: Paul H. Alvarado, Clerk: A. Mok. Proceedings reported by: Gena Eales, CSR 176.

Daniel B. Klausner, Esq., appearing for Cross-Complainant ALEXANDRE BAPA, sworn and examined,
No appearance by defaulted cross-defendants LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY "SINTEZ" (A RUSSIAN LLC), DIRECTLY AND DERIVATIVELY ON BEHALF OF SINTEZ, LLC (A DOMESTIC LLC) and SAMUR BAYAN,
Default of Cross-Defendants has been duly entered.
Court ordered Cross-Defendants Does 1-50 dismissed without prejudice.

After Evidence and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Cross-Complainant and against Defaulted Cross-Defendants as follows:

For cross-defendants LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY "SINTEZ" (A RUSSIAN LLC), DIRECTLY AND DERIVATIVELY ON BEHALF OF SINTEZ, LLC (A DOMESTIC LLC) and SAMUR BAYAN

For cross-defendant SAMUR BAYAN
Damages: $77,775.00

Pre-Judgment Interest: $0.00

Total Judgment in the amount of $18,511.85

Off Calendar Jan-20-2015 Continued To Dec-02-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And Petition For Stay Of This Action
Off Calendar Dec-05-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. MARTIN ZURADA OF VENARDI ZURADA LLP, TEL. NO. (925)937-3900 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
ATTY. DANIEL B. KLAUSNER, TEL. NO. (650) 867-1185 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; PLAINTIFF LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY "SINTEZ" DIRECTLY AND DERIVATIVELY ON BEHALF OF SINTEZ, LLC'S Motion To Compel Arbitration And Petition For Stay Of This Action-
The motion to compel arbitration is granted and the Court stays this action. The broadly phrased arbitration clause applies to the instant matter. Assuming that the court has the power to rule on the unconscionability issue, defendant fails to demonstrate unconscionability. Defendant does not present a declaration or any other evidence showing how the agreement was allegedly foisted upon defendant or that defendant could not understand the terms of the agreement. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR #5976 =302/ehg

MINI MINUTES:

PLAINTIFF LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY "SINTEZ" DEMURRER/PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION OF DEFENDANT BAPA'S CROSS-COMPLAINT is Off calendar. A First Amended Cross Complaint was filed on November 3, 2014. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =322/mjm

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-01-2014 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. MARTIN ZURADA OF THE VENARDI ZURADA LLP, TEL. NO. (925) 937-3900 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
ATTY. DANIEL B. KLAUSNER, TEL. NO. (650) 867-1185 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT ALEXANDRE BAPA's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained with 15 days leave to amend. Plaintiff fails to state facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action pursuant to C.C.P. 430.10(e). A preliminary injunction is an interim remedy and is not, in itself, a cause of action (see MaJor v. Miraverde Homeowners Ass'n (1992) 7 Cal.App.4th 618, 623) and Corporate Code 1281.8(b) requires that an application for a Preliminary Injunction "be accompanied by a complaint or copies of the demand for arbitration and any response thereto." Order signed. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/eg
